Name: Jamie
Score Notes:
Intro Page Misled the Direction:
-The opening narrative gave him an unusual sense of direction.
-It made him suspect malicious traffic or a security issue, which wasn’t aligned with the actual drill focus.
-He felt the clue in the intro was misleading and wasted his time.

Felt Out of Sync Compared to Other Drills:
-The scenario didn’t flow as smoothly as others he’d experienced.
-There were too many parallel threads, making it feel disjointed and overwhelming.

Confusion Due to Multiple Threads:
-Found it hard to decide which path to follow during the investigation.
-The numerous simultaneous conversations made it easy to lose track of the core issue.

Missed Key Detail on Infra Migration:
-He completely missed the part where Tanya mentioned the currency service was migrated.
-The communication lacked specificity—"currency service" should have been explicitly highlighted.

Coaching notes...
Just sharing my coaching tips for future sessions with Jamie from Trainline (based on the drill - memory lane on Friday)
I shared Hamed's talk on incident groundhog day
covering the 2 points - solo vs band player approach + therapeutic action that can turn into a diagnostic (rollback, restart etc)
These concepts would be good to run over with him in next set of drills - whereby we can practice these skills
https://uptimelabsio.slack.com/archives/D02NLS8K3M3/p1750020896637659
